title: Isonandra cambodiana
intro: Isonandra cambodiana, also known as the Cambodian Isonandra, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Isonandra cambodiana include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Isonandra cambodiana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make herbal teas, tonics, and capsules that are commonly used to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Isonandra cambodiana is classified as a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and belongs to the genus Isonandra. Within the Sapotaceae family, Isonandra cambodiana is part of a group of flowering plants known as the Sapindales order. The classification also includes the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. At the most basic level, the classification for Isonandra cambodiana includes the domain Eukarya.
